title: Pilosella × byzantina
intro: Pilosella × byzantina, also known as Byzan Meadow Rue or Butterfly Plant, is a hybrid perennial plant belonging to the Rosaceae family. Traditionally, Pilosella × byzantina has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, skin conditions, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pilosella × byzantina is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants, to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Pilosella × byzantina is a hybrid species within the dandelion genus, specifically belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is a cross between Pilosella officinarum and Pilosella eichleriana, two species of the Pilosella genus. As a hybrid, its classification is somewhat complex, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Pilosella genus within the Asteraceae family. The Pilosella × byzantina is also sometimes referred to as a cultivar, but its botanical classification remains as a hybrid species. Its classification is further supported by its unique characteristics and hybrid genetic makeup.
uses: Pilosella × byzantina, also known as false sowthistle, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids, which are believed to have therapeutic effects. In some cultures, Pilosella × byzantina is used to treat digestive issues, such as ulcers and diarrhea. Additionally, its extracts have been studied for their potential to reduce oxidative stress and improve cardiovascular health. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pilosella × byzantina as a medicinal plant.
benefits: Pilosella × byzantina, also known as the Olympic Mountain mouse-ear, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is believed to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds and infections. constituents: Pilosella × byzantina, also known as false sowthistle, contains a range of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpene lactones. These sesquiterpene lactones have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to their potential medicinal applications. parts: Pilosella × byzantina, also known as Aurora consolida, is a hybrid plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern European folklore.
